seoTitle: Hexham vs Hickeys Creek — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Hexham and Hickeys Creek. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Hexham vs Hickeys Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hexham and Hickeys Creek.
heading: Hexham vs Hickeys Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hexham, New South Wales and Hickeys Creek, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hexham has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Hickeys Creek (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hexham or Hickeys Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hexham has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Hickeys Creek scores 23/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hexham scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Hickeys Creek scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hexham or Hickeys Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hexham has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
